chartSurfaceMissing: packages/krate/charts/Chart.yaml currently has vela-core and kyverno dependencies but no jitsi-meet dependency
packages/krate/charts/values.yaml currently has no top-level jitsi section
packages/krate/charts/templates/networkpolicy.yaml currently has only generic namespace ingress/egress and no JVB UDP 10000-specific behavior
existingReusableJitsiSurfaces: packages/krate/core/src/resource-model.js already defines JitsiMeetProvider, JitsiMeetingTemplate, JitsiMeeting, and JitsiRecording resource definitions
packages/krate/cli/src/mcp-server.js already exposes Jitsi meeting MCP tools and uses KRATE_JITSI_JWT_SECRET for local JWT signing fallback
packages/krate/cli/tests/jitsi-mcp-tools.test.js already covers the CLI/MCP Jitsi meeting tool contract
packages/krate/web/app/api/orgs/[org]/jitsi and packages/krate/web/tests/jitsi-structure.test.js already provide web/API Jitsi surfaces from adjacent work
planningImplications: Do not duplicate controller, CLI, or web Jitsi work that exists or belongs to adjacent issues
Anchor issue #623 implementation on Helm dependency, chart values, chart CRDs, secret wiring, network policy/media behavior, and package validation
Update or extend package validation only where it verifies chart/deployment artifacts, not to reimplement application controllers

issueScope: Add jitsi-meet as a Helm dependency with condition jitsi.install
Add jitsi values for internal deployment and external deployment connection mode
Configure web, prosody JWT auth, jicofo, jvb, and optional jibri recording values
Add charts/crds/jitsi-resources.yaml for JitsiMeetProvider, JitsiMeetingTemplate, JitsiMeeting, JitsiParticipant if required by docs, and JitsiRecording
Represent Jitsi media traffic on UDP 10000 in network policy or rendered chart behavior
Manage JWT and webhook secrets without committing literal production secret values
Verify pods/config/health expectations through Helm/package render gates and documented operational checks
qualityGateCommands: helm template krate packages/krate/charts --set jitsi.install=false --set jitsi.external.enabled=true --set jitsi.external.url=https://meet.example.test --set jitsi.external.jwtAppId=krate
renderAssertions: Chart.yaml includes jitsi-meet dependency from https://jitsi-contrib.github.io/jitsi-helm/ with alias jitsi-subchart and condition jitsi.install
Default values include jitsi.install and jitsi.external.enabled with clear precedence
Internal render supports web, prosody JWT, jicofo, jvb UDP 10000, and optional jibri values
External render does not require in-cluster Jitsi workloads and exposes external connection settings for Krate
Rendered manifests do not expose literal JWT app secrets or webhook secrets from committed defaults
Network policy or service behavior permits JVB UDP 10000 media traffic when internal Jitsi is installed
breakpointPolicy: sparse: only pause when the reuse audit finds a conflicting partial Jitsi chart surface or an unresolved internal-vs-external deployment decision
nonGoals: Do not implement Jitsi controllers, web management UI, meeting iframe, agent sidecars, or recording workflows beyond chart/CRD/deployment contracts required by issue #623
Do not deploy to a live cluster unless a later run receives explicit maintainer approval
Do not commit real JWT app secrets, webhook secrets, cloud credentials, or environment-specific private values
